title: Abdominal Pain in Early Pregnancy - Ectopic Pregnancy
chief_complaint: 28-year-old G2P1 at 6 weeks with left lower abdominal pain and spotting for 2 days. Pain worsening. Positive home pregnancy test. No prior ultrasound.

physical_exam.pelvic: Left adnexal tenderness, cervical motion tenderness, uterus slightly enlarged

hidden_data.us_pelvis: Empty uterus, left adnexal mass 3cm with ring of fire on Doppler, free fluid in pelvis

key_learning_points: hCG >1500 + empty uterus = ectopic until proven
Methotrexate if: stable, mass <3.5cm, no cardiac activity, hCG <5000
Surgery if unstable, failed MTX, or contraindications

patient_responses.pain: Sharp pain on left side, getting worse

feedback.correct_message: Excellent! Ectopic pregnancy needs urgent management.
feedback.incorrect_message: This is ectopic pregnancy. The empty uterus + hCG >2000 + adnexal mass confirm it.
feedback.attending_note: Ectopic: If stable + mass <3.5cm + no cardiac activity + hCG <5000: MTX 50mg/m² IM single dose. Follow hCG days 4 and 7. Surgery if unstable or MTX contraindicated. Rhogam if Rh-negative.
